addressed: hive-column-injection: unsanitized partition column names in HiveEngineSpec.where_latest_partition() and PrestoEngineSpec.where_latest_partition()
structured_output.reasoning: Partition column names from database.get_indexes() (Hive metastore metadata) were passed directly to sqlalchemy.Column() without validation. A sql_lab user could create a table with malicious partition column names that would flow unsanitized into query construction when another user views that table via select_star. Fixed by adding SAFE_IDENTIFIER_REGEX (^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$) validation in both PrestoEngineSpec and HiveEngineSpec where_latest_partition methods. If validation fails, the method returns None and logs a warning. Tests confirm malicious names are rejected and safe names continue to work.
